verdict: Step 2: elementary antipodal lemma T'<=N/2 (proved) + R-025 (K_floor<=T') + packing (N<=40) => K_floor<=20<20.55=K* => R_lead<1 for C_full incl non-lattice (THIN x1.026, the A_ext fallback): the off-diagonal C_full closure is PROVED, not a conjecture. The actual K_floor~1-3 across all families (T' up to N/2=20 a loose proxy) => EXT (K_floor~3) is a MARGIN UPGRADE (x1.026->~x5), NOT a closure blocker; stays T2. Corrects res5_031 'lattice strictly worst' -> actual ~3 across all classes.

claims.name: antipodal_lemma_Tprime_le_Nhalf
claims.detail: (elementary lemma: x in C_t => -x not in C_t for t!=0, so each antipodal pair gives <=1 to a sum-circle => T'<=N/2; verified for every antipodal family (random, latitude-ring, Z^3 shell))

claims.name: offdiag_Cfull_thin_closure_proved
claims.detail: (K_floor<=T'<=N/2<=n_pack/2=20<20.55=K*; R_lead<=23.2*(1+20)*2e-3=0.974<1 (margin x1.026). Off-diagonal for C_full (incl non-lattice) is PROVED via the antipodal lemma + R-025 + packing -- THIN (A_ext fallback), not a conjecture)

claims.name: actual_Kfloor_small_Tprime_loose
claims.detail: (actual K_floor in [0.85,2.47] across random/latitude-ring/rich-shell; max T'=20 (=N/2 for rings) yet K_floor<=2.47: the L-infinity occupancy T' is a LOOSE proxy for the L-2 floor K_floor)

claims.name: ext_is_margin_upgrade_not_blocker
claims.detail: (EXT actual-floor margin: R_lead=23.2*(1+2.47)*2e-3=0.161 (margin x6.2) vs loose fallback x1.026. EXT (K_floor~3 maximal) UPGRADES the thin fallback to comfortable; it is NOT a closure blocker. Stays T2 (operator 2026-06-10: register, do not promote); tight L-2 bound = promotion path)

claims.name: quantitative_sanity_ext
claims.detail: (K*=20.55; actual max K_floor=2.47<13 (EXT bound, loose) <20.6=K*; antipodal lemma holds; thin closure R_lead<1. EXT falsifier: an admissible Q with K_floor>13. Correction to res5_031 'lattice strictly worst' headline recorded -- actual extremal ~3 across all classes, T' loose proxy)
